pattern: Greedy interval scheduling: sort by end time and keep every interval that starts no earlier than the last kept end, counting the discards.
hints: Minimizing removals is equivalent to maximizing the number of non-overlapping intervals you keep — a classic greedy scheduling problem.
Sort intervals by their end time; among any set of overlapping intervals, keeping the one that ends earliest leaves the most room for future intervals.
Iterate through end-sorted intervals and greedily keep each one whose start is at least the end of the last kept interval, counting every interval you skip.

edge_inputs.description: one interval overlaps with two others
all intervals identical, remove all but one
no overlaps at all, remove nothing
intervals that only touch at an endpoint are not overlapping
one giant interval engulfs many small ones
intervals sorted by start not end — must re-sort
two fully overlapping intervals, remove one

stress.description: large random interval set with varied overlap density
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    intervals = []
    for _ in range(n):
        a = rng.randint(-50000, 50000)
        b = rng.randint(a + 1, min(a + 1000, 50000))
        intervals.append([a, b])
    return [intervals]
